new_lines: pub(crate) fn update_ranges(&mut self, ranges: Vec<u32>) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error> {
"Failed to send range update to query task: {}",
let mut scheduled_file_keys_to_attempts: HashMap<String, u32> = HashMap::new();
// Tests (and any future code) that use
// depend on the checkpoint task state update
// already being enqueued by the time the
"Triggering dirty document checkpoint for file {} (scheduled at {:?})",
"Failed to send EnforceAllChangesCheckpointed message to megaprocess thread: {err}"
"Triggering dirty document checkpoint log for file {} (scheduled at {:?})",
// During rollout, a file may be attempted repeatedly and exceed max attempts.
// This means the file will not be checkpointed/deleted from dirty_documents until scheduled_file_keys_to_attempts
// is reset (for example, with a deploy). This removal ensures that, if the flag value
// for a specific file is changed to true, the file will actually be checkpointed/deleted.
// This is mostly applicable in devenv01 but will help us get realistic metrics throughout
// rollout from other environments as well.
// Handle first tick with delay, then switch to regular interval
warn!("Failed to send dirty docs to checkpoint task: {}", e);
"Dirty document query took {:?}, which is longer than the query interval",
debug!("Query task received range update: {} ranges", new_ranges.len());
// Query immediately when new ranges are added to avoid possible 2x interval delay
debug!("Querying {} newly added ranges immediately after update", added_ranges.len());
warn!("Failed to send dirty docs to checkpoint task after range update: {}", e);
"Exceeded max dirty_document checkpoint attempts for file {}, skipping",
// Already exceeded max attempts, skip silently so we don't keep logging
// and incrementing the metric repeatedly for this file
debug!("Retrying dirty document query for range {}", range_index);
// Exhausted retries for this range, move to next
warn_without_sentry!("Exhausted backoff attempts for dirty document query for range {}, skipping", range_index);
while let Some((file_key, result, dirtied_at)) = deletion_rx.recv().await {
"Failed to delete dirty document after failed checkpoint: {err}"
// drop the channel so sends will fail
.expect("Expected get_dirty_documents to be called multiple times despite send failures");
"DirtyDocumentCheckpoints should still be healthy after send failures"
let mut scheduled_file_keys_to_attempts: HashMap<String, u32> = HashMap::new();
// Create a dirty doc that's way overdue (dirtied more than 5 minutes + jitter ago)
let overdue_time = SystemTime::now() - Duration::from_secs(10 * 60); // 10 minutes ago
// Overdue document should be scheduled immediately (at current time)
"Overdue document should be scheduled immediately or in the past"
let mut scheduled_file_keys_to_attempts: HashMap<String, u32> = HashMap::new();
.expect("query task should run and call get_dirty_documents");
// The query task may wake up and process range 0 before seeing range 1, so we need a mock expectation for range 0
// It's ok if both ranges are queried, we mainly want to confirm that the query task processes range 1 immediately.
.expect("query task should run and call get_dirty_documents for range 1");
"Range update should be processed quickly, took {:?}",
.expect("Expected query task to recover after a query failure");
(1000 * 60 * 60).into(), // very long interval to avoid standard query
// Wait for success result on deletion channel. The spawned task
// sends ClearFileKeyAttempts to checkpoint_tx before sending to
// deletion_tx, so by the time we receive here, the clear message
// is already queued ahead of any ScheduleDirtyDocs we send next.
// Schedule the same file again. With max_checkpoint_attempts=2, it schedules normally
"Expected third EnforceAllChangesCheckpointed (attempts should have been cleared), got {:?}",
// Should not be rescheduled — no second EnforceAllChangesCheckpointed
"Non-retriable error should not reschedule the checkpoint"
